CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, str1 VARCHAR(100), str2 VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (2, 'DB', 'MonetDB is a database');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (3, 'DB', 'This is a test for MonetDB');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (4, 'test', 'This is a complex test for utility');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (5, 'notfound', 'Searching through strings');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (6, 'Monet', 'MonetDB is efficient and fast');
SELECT position(str1 IN str2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT position(str1 IN str2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT position(str1 IN str2) FROM test_strings WHERE str2 LIKE '%MonetDB%';
SELECT position('test' IN str2) FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE str1 = 'DB') ORDER BY id;
SELECT s.id, position(s.str1 IN s.str2) AS pos FROM test_strings s INNER JOIN test_strings s2 ON s.id = s2.id WHERE s.str1 = 'DB';
SELECT DISTINCT s.id FROM test_strings s WHERE position(s.str1 IN s.str2) = 0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE position('notfound' IN str2) = 0;
SELECT MAX(position(str1 IN str2)) FROM test_strings;
WITH t AS (SELECT id, position(str1 IN str2) AS pos FROM test_strings) SELECT * FROM t WHERE pos > 0;
SELECT position('Monet' IN (SELECT str2 FROM test_strings WHERE id = 6));
